titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the John Knowles Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 7186483.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday September 5, 2020 at 03:12

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The John Knowles Papers document the career and personal life of American author John Knowles between 1954 and 2000. The papers provide insight into Knowles's career as an author and consist of correspondence, writings, notebooks, scrapbooks, printed material, personal papers, audiovisual media, and other papers. The papers also include a portrait (pencil on paper) of Knowles by Don Bachardy. There is some material relating to Knowles's friendships with Truman Capote and Thornton Wilder. Various drafts of his writings, including A Separate Peace and its companion novel Peace Breaks Out, reflect Knowles's creative life.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Box 18 (audiocassettes, CD): Use of originals is restricted. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The John Knowles Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: John Knowles Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and in some instances minimal organization.

The finding aid for this collection is compiled from individual preliminary lists for each acquisition that were created at or around the time of receipt by the library. The preliminary lists were migrated to comply with current archival descriptive standards and merged into a single file in 2007-2008. As part of the migration, modifications were made to the formatting of individual lists; however, the content of the lists was neither modified nor verified.

As a rule, descriptive information found in the Collection Contents section is drawn in large part from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This collection includes materials previously identified by the following call number: Uncat MSS 396.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

bioghist.head: John Knowles (1926-2001)
bioghist.p: John Knowles was an American novelist best known for his first novel A Separate Peace .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The John Knowles Papers document the career and personal life of American author John Knowles between 1954 and 2000. The papers provide insight into Knowles's career as an author and consist of correspondence, writings, notebooks, scrapbooks, printed material, personal papers, audio-visual media, and other papers. The papers also include a portrait of Knowles by Don Bachardy. There is some material relating to Knowles's friendship with Truman Capote and Thornton Wilder. Various drafts of his writings, including A Separate Peace and its companion novel Peace Breaks Out , reflect Knowles's creative process.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into one grouping: I. March 2002 Acquisition.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Not arranged in any particular order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, writings, notebooks, scrapbooks, printed material, personal papers, audio-visual material, a framed portrait, and other papers by and relating to John Knowles.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 18 (audiocassettes): Use of originals is restricted. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: The Private Life of Axie Reed : typescripts
The Private Life of Axie Reed : typescripts
A Vein of Riches : typescripts and drafts
A Vein of Riches : typescripts and drafts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Learning and Teaching; Rapture of the Deep; A Special Time; a Special School; Intro to Collection of Short Stories; Worthy of the Sea; Hawthorne, an Introduction; The Interruption; The Yale Mystique; Egypt on the Brink of the Six Day War; and writings of others

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes diaries and lecture notes; essays, stories, and fragments of various works

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diaries; reviews of Peace Breaks Out ; essays and reviews by Knowles

did.unittitle: Yale University Class of 1959, The American Century: Our Half (50th Reunion, 1999). Includes essay by Knowles: "Writing at Yale...And Beyond."
Audiovisual Materials of A Separate Peace

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fifteen of the audiocassettes are commercial. They have not been digitized.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 6 notebooks containing notes and drafts; First draft, typescript, revised; Second draft, revised; Third draft, revised; Photocopy, with revisions; draft of letter to publisher from author, 1973 Oct 19

did.unittitle: Indian Summer : 5 notebooks; typescript; revised pages
Double Vision: Thoughts from Abroad : typescript; 18 notebooks
Double Vision: Thoughts from Abroad : research notes, clippings and travel literature
Phineas : Six Stories: typescript; original and carbon; 4 proofs; related notes and other writings
Morning in Antibes : Ms.; typescript; 4 notebooks; French newspapers
Too Far to Walk (dramatic treatment): Notebook containing ms. and notes; Typescript (notes)
Page proof of John Hersey novel

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Short articles; Idea memos; An Anglo-American Dream; Book reviews; The Count in his Castle; Forquet; France; The Great City Comes to Virginia; Jack Knowles vacation; The Heart of France; How to be Sick Abroad; The Incomplete Skin Diver; The Last Monks of Byzantium; Natural America; News; Not Taking Things So Hard; Radicofani; Sweden; Thought for the Milestone Year; Wedding in Old France; The Young Writer's Enemies and Friends

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Cure; Delay en Route; Leaving Home; Night Music; Summer Street; Traffic Control on the Grand Canal

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Reading of the Will; Publishers Weekly: press article; Paradises I have Known; Ohlik, Giza: School at the Frontier, review; The Heart of France; The Outer Islands of Hawaii; France Fumbles with the American Way; Crossing the Snow Bridge; The Anchor Man; Newsday columns; A Winter in Egypt and The Heart of France

did.unittitle: Packard, George V.: That Grail Song, Sam, One More Time, uncorrected proof
Framed portrait of John Knowles by Don Bachardy
"John Knowles - on Contemporary Fiction", Princeton University, event poster
